title: hypericum horizontale
intro: Hypericum horizontale, also known as creeping St. John's Wort, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of hyperforin, a compound that has been used to treat mild depression and anxiety due to its antidepressant properties. The health benefits of Hypericum horizontale include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve sleep quality, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of stress, insomnia, and mild depression.
